sn12.38:1.2: (the Blessed One said,) “Monks, what one intends, what one arranges, and what one obsesses about: This is a support for the stationing of consciousness.
sn12.38:1.3: There being a support, there is a landing of consciousness.
sn12.38:1.4: When that consciousness lands and grows, there is the production of renewed becoming in the future.
sn12.38:1.5: When there is the production of renewed becoming in the future, there is future birth, aging-&-death, sorrow, lamentation, pain, distress, & despair.
sn12.38:1.6: Such is the origination of this entire mass of suffering & stress.
sn12.38:2.1: “If one doesn’t intend and doesn’t arrange, but one still obsesses (about something), this is a support for the stationing of consciousness.
sn12.38:2.2: There being a support, there is a landing of consciousness.
sn12.38:2.3: When that consciousness lands and grows, there is the production of renewed becoming in the future.
sn12.38:2.4: When there is the production of renewed becoming in the future, there is future birth, aging-&-death, sorrow, lamentation, pain, distress, & despair.
sn12.38:2.5: Such (too) is the origination of this entire mass of suffering & stress.
sn12.38:3.1: “But when one doesn’t intend, arrange, or obsess (about anything), there is no support for the stationing of consciousness.
sn12.38:3.2: There being no support, there is no landing of consciousness.
sn12.38:3.3: When that consciousness doesn’t land & grow, there is no production of renewed becoming in the future.
sn12.38:3.4: When there is no production of renewed becoming in the future, there is no future birth, aging-&-death, sorrow, lamentation, pain, distress, or despair.
sn12.38:3.5: Such is the cessation of this entire mass of suffering & stress.”
